# 🚀 Pull Request Guidelines — ICNARI 2026 Conference Website

Thanks for contributing to the ICNARI 2026 Conference Website! 🎓  
To keep the codebase clean, scalable, and production-ready, please follow these guidelines before submitting a Pull Request.

---

## 📌 Before Creating a PR

- 🔍 Check if an issue already exists for your change
- 🧠 Understand the project structure (see docs/DEVELOPER_GUIDE.md)
- ⚠️ Do NOT make unnecessary changes (especially configs, env, or unrelated files)
- 🧪 Test your changes locally (both frontend & backend if applicable)

---

## ❗ Important Rules

### 1. No Unrelated Changes
- Do NOT remove or modify:
  - `.env.example`
  - Docker / Jenkins configs
  - Lock files (unless required)
- Avoid formatting or refactoring unrelated code

---

### 2. Backend Rules (Express + MongoDB)

- Do NOT change API schema unless explicitly required
- Follow existing route structure (`/speaker`, `/papers`, etc.)
- Use proper validation and error handling
- Protected routes must use existing JWT middleware (`token` header)

---

### 3. Frontend Rules (React + Vite)

- Maintain existing UI consistency (Tailwind + Chakra UI)
- Avoid breaking existing pages
- Use reusable components where possible
- API calls must use `VITE_API_URL`

---

### 4. File & Code Standards

- Use meaningful variable and function names
- Keep code clean and modular
- Follow existing folder structure
- Remove console.logs before submitting PR

---

## 🧾 PR Title Format

Use clear and structured titles:
